top_stories.title: Okta extends AI agent security to Amazon Bedrock, opens platform to rival identity providers
top_stories.summary: Okta expanded its Okta for AI Agents platform to support any agent ecosystem, enterprise resource, and identity provider, including a new integration with Amazon Bedrock. The platform extension also opens compatibility to rival identity providers, broadening Okta's AI agent security coverage beyond its own ecosystem.

top_stories.title: Palo Alto Networks Introduces Idira: The Next-Generation Identity Security Platform Built for the AI Enterprise
top_stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks has introduced Idira, a next-generation identity security platform designed for AI-driven enterprises. The platform targets identity security use cases relevant to IAM professionals, representing a significant new entrant in the identity security market from a major cybersecurity vendor.

top_stories.title: Google lets Workspace admins apply one policy across all SAML apps
top_stories.summary: Google has updated Context-Aware Access (CAA) in Google Workspace to support a default policy assignment that automatically applies to all SAML-based applications without a specific policy. This universal baseline policy allows Workspace admins to enforce a consistent security posture across all SAML SSO-integrated applications simultaneously.

top_stories.title: Akamai to acquire LayerX for $205 million
top_stories.summary: Akamai has entered a definitive agreement to acquire browser security vendor LayerX for $205 million. The acquisition extends Akamai's zero trust security portfolio with browser-based AI usage controls and secure enterprise browser technology targeting SaaS and AI application access.

top_stories.title: Keycard helps developers secure autonomous AI agents with scoped access
top_stories.summary: Keycard has launched Keycard for Multi-Agent Apps, a platform extension that provides delegated, session-based access control for autonomous AI agent systems. The solution gives each agent its own identity, scopes access per task, and enables full attribution of actions across agents, users, and systems.

news.title: Inside the REMUS Infostealer: Session Theft, MaaS, and Rapid Evolution
news.summary: The REMUS infostealer malware targets browser sessions and authentication tokens as a Malware-as-a-Service (MaaS) offering, bypassing traditional password-based authentication controls. Flare's analysis details how REMUS has evolved to focus on session theft, which undermines MFA and SSO protections by stealing already-authenticated session credentials.

news.title: Cisco patches another actively exploited SD-WAN zero-day (CVE-2026-20182)
news.summary: Cisco patched an actively exploited authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-20182) in its Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and SD-WAN Manager, attributed to a highly sophisticated threat actor. The flaw stems from a flawed peering authentication mechanism and affects both on-premises and cloud deployments.

news.title: Unpatched Microsoft Exchange Server vulnerability exploited (CVE-2026-42897)
news.summary: Microsoft has warned that a critical XSS vulnerability (CVE-2026-42897) in on-premises Microsoft Exchange Server (Subscription Edition RTM, 2019, and 2016) is actively being exploited, with a permanent fix still in development. The vulnerability allows unauthorized attackers to perform spoofing over a network, and Microsoft has issued temporary mitigations while Exchange Online remains unaffected.

news.title: Zombie linkages are keeping expired domains trusted for years
news.summary: Researchers from USC and the University of Twente identified "zombie linkages" — trust records that persist in systems like Web PKI, Maven Central, and Ethereum Name Service after domain ownership changes. Expired domains that are re-registered by new owners can inherit the original owner's trusted credentials, creating potential for unauthorized access or impersonation.

news.title: CISA Adds Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 to KEV After Admin Access Exploits
news.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-20182, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies are required to remediate the issue by May 17, 2026, following confirmed active exploitation enabling admin access.

news.title: The Next Cybersecurity Challenge May Be Verifying AI Agents
news.summary: The article addresses the emerging cybersecurity challenge of verifying and authenticating AI agents as they become more prevalent in enterprise environments. It covers the need for trusted identity standards and runtime controls specific to non-human AI identities.

news.title: Palo Alto Networks Idira: A next-gen platform for human, agentic & machine identity security
news.summary: Palo Alto Networks has unveiled Idira, a new identity security platform designed to address human, agentic, and machine identity security. The platform is positioned as a next-generation, AI-era solution for identity management and security.

news.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
news.summary: Cisco has released patches for a maximum-severity authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0) in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and SD-WAN Manager that is being actively exploited. The flaw affects peering authentication mechanisms, allowing attackers to gain administrative access without valid credentials.

news.title: Regional routing for AWS access portals: Implementing custom vanity domains for IAM Identity Center
news.summary: AWS has launched multi-Region replication for IAM Identity Center, enabling customers to implement regional routing with custom vanity domains for access portals. The feature allows workforce users to access a single portal for AWS accounts and applications with improved regional availability and custom domain support.

news.title: Identity breaches are on the rise, and are only getting worse - so how can your business stay safe?
news.summary: CyberArk has issued a warning about the growing threat of identity breaches, highlighting AI agents' access to sensitive files as an emerging risk. The company is advocating for a unified, automated identity security approach to address the increasing frequency and severity of identity-related incidents.

news.title: Interview: AI optimism and upskilling for a shifting job market
news.summary: Okta's chief marketing officer Shannon Duffy shared views on AI's potential as a productivity tool and job market equalizer in an interview. The discussion focuses on workforce upskilling and AI optimism rather than Okta's IAM products or services.

news.title: Most Organizations Now Use AI Agents for Sensitive Security Tasks
news.summary: A Semperis study found that most organizations are now deploying AI agents for sensitive security tasks, with 74% believing AI will increase attacks on identity infrastructure. The research highlights growing concern among IAM and security professionals about AI-driven threats targeting identity systems.

news.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft released patches for 138 security vulnerabilities, including 30 rated Critical, covering flaws in DNS, Netlogon, and other products. Among the 138 flaws, 61 are privilege escalation vulnerabilities, which are directly relevant to IAM and access control concerns.

news.title: New Semperis Study Reveals AI's Effects on the Identity Attack Surface
news.summary: Semperis released a study examining how AI agents are expanding the identity attack surface, finding organizations are granting AI systems access to critical infrastructure before establishing adequate security controls. The research specifically addresses risks to Active Directory, Microsoft Entra ID, and Okta environments as AI adoption accelerates.

news.title: Keeper Security Delivers Structured Governance for Humans and AI Agents with New PAM Approval Workflows in KeeperPAM
news.summary: Keeper Security has launched new PAM Approval Workflows within its KeeperPAM platform, introducing structured governance and approval-based controls for privileged access. The new Keeper Workflow capability is designed to provide administrators oversight for both human and AI agent privileged access within a zero-trust, zero-knowledge framework.

news.title: Worm Redux: Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Infections Bite Supply Chain
news.summary: Hundreds of npm packages in the TanStack open source ecosystem have been infected by a self-propagating, credential-stealing worm attributed to TeamPCP. The worm spreads through supply chain infection and is designed to steal credentials from affected systems.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. He admitted involvement in SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from users across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to targeted accounts.
